steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to compare the curviness of the Mississippi and Neale rivers using the data table. The answer must state which river has a higher sinuosity ratio — the direct measure of curviness.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: states the Neale has a greater sinuosity ratio than the Mississippi — this reverses the data. ✗
  • Choice B: describes how sinuosity is calculated rather than comparing the two rivers. ✗
  • Choice C: makes a claim about river length without comparing sinuosity ratios directly. ✗
  • Choice D: states the Mississippi's sinuosity ratio is greater than the Neale's, directly identifying which river is curvier in line with the data. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
